summary.quantification_method: Internal standard quantification using spiked-in stable isotope-labeled (SIL) peptides

primary_variables.matched_definition: Permanent loss of function through full gene knockout (for example CRISPR KO or deletion).
evidence: examine how the expression levels of epitranscriptomic RWE proteins are perturbed by genetic depletion of m6A writer and eraser proteins
isogenic cells with the catalytic subunit of the m6A writer complex (i.e., METTL3) and m6A eraser proteins (i.e., ALKBH5 and FTO) being genetically ablated
ALKBH5−/−, FTO−/−, and METTL3−/− cells relative to parental HEK293T cells
catalytic subunit of the major m6A writer complex (i.e., METTL3) being individually ablated
genes encoding m6A eraser proteins (i.e., ALKBH5, FTO) ... being individually ablated
genes encoding m6A eraser proteins (i.e., ALKBH5, FTO) ... being individually ablated

replicate_hints: Three replicate samples were initially prepared
only two replicates of METTL3-/- cell samples were analyzed
averaged from the quantification results of 2-3 biological replicates
multiplex_channel_hints: incorporating stable isotope-labeled (SIL) peptides as internal or surrogate standards

evidence: relative expression levels of 117, 119, and 118 RWE proteins in ALKBH5, FTO, and METTL3 knockout cells with respect to parental HEK293T cells
relative expression levels of 117, 119, and 118 RWE proteins in ALKBH5, FTO, and METTL3 knockout cells with respect to parental HEK293T cells
relative expression levels of 117, 119, and 118 RWE proteins in ALKBH5, FTO, and METTL3 knockout cells with respect to parental HEK293T cells

quantification_strategy: Internal standard quantification using spiked-in stable isotope-labeled (SIL) peptides

base_candidates_ranked.reason: While the data is PRM (targeted), the quantification is relative (LFQ-like) using SIL peptides as internal standards rather than a multiplexed isobaric or SILAC approach.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment explicitly uses liquid chromatography-parallel-reaction monitoring (LC-PRM) for targeted quantification.
The method incorporates stable isotope-labeled (SIL) peptides as internal or surrogate standards spiked into the samples.
